scenarios.query:
Audit this architecture scan. Declared rules (docs/architecture.md): handlers -> services -> repos -> db; no skip or upward imports; no cycles.
```text
violations:
  services/payouts.go   imports  handlers/render.go
  handlers/users.go     imports  repos/users_repo.go   (skips services)
cycles:
  services/accounts.go -> services/ledger.go -> services/accounts.go
```
expected_behavior: Flags the upward import (services -> handlers) against the declared direction
Flags the skip-layer import (handlers directly to repos, bypassing services)
Flags the services cycle and recommends breaking it (extract shared concept or invert an edge)
Recommends encoding the rules as an automated fitness function so violations fail CI

scenarios.query:
Audit this scan. Declared rule (ADR-7): bounded contexts `catalog` and `checkout` may only communicate via published events; no shared DB.
```text
findings:
  checkout/inventory_sync.py reads table catalog_products directly (shared DB)
  checkout/api.py imports catalog/internal/pricing_rules.py
```
expected_behavior: Flags the shared-database access as cross-context coupling violating ADR-7 (recommend the published event/API contract instead)
Flags the reach-in to another context's internals (catalog/internal) as boundary violation
Frames both against the declared rule rather than generic style preference

scenarios.query:
Audit this architecture scan. Declared rule: ui -> app -> domain -> infra, no cycles.
```text
violations: none
cycles: none
fan-in/fan-out (top): domain/types.py fan-in 51 fan-out 2; app/use_cases.py fan-in 12 fan-out 9
```
expected_behavior: Reports no findings (conforms to the declared architecture)
Recognizes high fan-in with low fan-out (domain/types.py) as a healthy shared kernel, not a god module
Does not invent rules or violations the declared architecture doesn't contain
